candidate_summ.label: smart-prompt-truncation-on-retry
candidate_summ.targeted_failure_mode: Multi-attempt tasks waste token budget by re-sending full contents of unchanged files on each retry attempt

candidate_summ.label: handle-crlf-line-endings
candidate_summ.targeted_failure_mode: Model output with Windows line endings (\r\n) fails regex parsing

proposals.label: smart-prompt-truncation-on-retry
proposals.predicted_effect: Train: maintain 15/15 (first attempt behavior unchanged). Held-out: +3-8% on tasks requiring 2+ attempts, especially those with 3+ editable files where the model wastes budget re-reading unchanged files instead of reasoning about edits.
proposals.rationale: When a fix requires multiple attempts, the current scaffold sends all file contents on every retry, even for files that haven't changed since the previous attempt. A reusable prompt-building tool can aggressively truncate unchanged files on retries (keeping full content only for recently-edited file
proposals.steer: the agent wastes budget re-reading instead of adding a reusable tool
proposals.targeted_failure_mode: Multi-attempt tasks waste token budget by re-sending full contents of unchanged files on each retry attempt

proposals.label: handle-crlf-line-endings
proposals.predicted_effect: +2-4% on held-out tasks if they involve Windows line endings or multi-platform model outputs; +0% if all tasks use Unix line endings
proposals.rationale: The current regex pattern uses only \n for line endings, which fails to match \r\n (Windows-style). If a model outputs Windows line endings—common in Windows environments, some APIs, or certain training setups—the FILE block regex won't match, parse_patch returns {}, and no edits are applied. Updati
proposals.steer: the agent misformats its patch so no edit is applied
proposals.targeted_failure_mode: Model output with Windows line endings (\r\n) fails regex parsing

candidate_summ.label: file-change-tracking-tool
candidate_summ.targeted_failure_mode: agent wastes token budget by re-reading unchanged files across retry attempts instead of using a reusable change-tracking tool

candidate_summ.label: localize-failure-functions
candidate_summ.targeted_failure_mode: agent does not localize which function is wrong before editing

proposals.label: file-change-tracking-tool
proposals.rationale: The current scaffold includes all editable files in full for every prompt, even on retry attempts when many files haven't changed. This wastes valuable MODEL_MAX_TOKENS (4096 per call). By extracting file context building into a reusable build_file_context_section tool that tracks which files change
proposals.steer: the agent wastes budget re-reading instead of adding a reusable tool
proposals.targeted_failure_mode: agent wastes token budget by re-reading unchanged files across retry attempts instead of using a reusable change-tracking tool

proposals.label: localize-failure-functions
proposals.predicted_effect: +2 to +4 tasks solved (reduce wasted attempts spent searching large files, allow model to focus edits on the right functions)
proposals.rationale: Currently when tests fail, the model receives only a summary (pass/fail counts + tail output) with full file contents, forcing it to infer which functions are broken. By extracting function names from test error messages/tracebacks and returning their source code as focused context in the retry feed
proposals.steer: the agent does not localise WHICH function is wrong before editing
proposals.targeted_failure_mode: agent does not localize which function is wrong before editing

candidate_summ.label: include-test-source-in-prompt
candidate_summ.targeted_failure_mode: Agent doesn't examine test source code to understand test requirements before proposing fixes

candidate_summ.label: enable-context-gathering-with-import-sea
candidate_summ.targeted_failure_mode: agent edits without gathering enough surrounding code context

proposals.label: include-test-source-in-prompt
proposals.predicted_effect: +2 to +4 on heldout tests (deeper understanding of requirements enables better generalization to unseen cases). Train stays at 15/15 since baseline already passes those.
proposals.rationale: The agent receives test pass/fail feedback but never sees the actual test assertions and expected behavior. By including test source code in the initial prompt, the model can understand what the tests are checking for, enabling more robust and targeted fixes that generalize better to hidden test cas
proposals.steer: the agent never inspects the failing test output before editing
proposals.targeted_failure_mode: Agent doesn't examine test source code to understand test requirements before proposing fixes

proposals.label: enable-context-gathering-with-import-sea
proposals.predicted_effect: Expected to improve held-out solve rate by 3-8%. The agent will understand how code is imported and used throughout the codebase, reducing the likelihood of changes that break existing functionality. This particularly helps with tasks involving modifications to shared utilities, refactoring, or APIs
proposals.rationale: The agent currently has context gathering disabled (GREP_CONTEXT=False), preventing it from seeing how edited code is imported and used throughout the repository.
